people.text: Ben wants a website to practise maths for school. He needs short exercises with instant answers and would prefer something he can use on his phone during bus journeys. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

Clara is planning a small garden and wants clear advice for beginners. She needs pictures of plants and information about which ones grow well in pots. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

Daniel wants to learn basic guitar at home. He needs free video lessons and would like to choose songs that are suitable for complete beginners. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

Nina is interested in world news but finds long articles difficult. She wants short reports with simple vocabulary and audio so she can listen too. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

Tom wants to compare prices before buying computer equipment. He needs user reviews and a tool that shows whether prices have recently gone up or down. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

texts.text: MathsMinute offers quick practice questions for secondary school students. Each activity takes about five minutes, gives answers immediately and works smoothly on mobile screens. Topics include fractions, percentages and graphs, and users can save favourite exercises for later revision. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

GreenStart Garden is aimed at people growing plants for the first time. It has step-by-step guides, clear photos and a special section on flowers, herbs and vegetables that do well in containers. The site also explains watering and sunlight needs. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

GuitarNow has hundreds of video lessons, but most require a monthly payment. The free section includes advanced techniques and interviews with musicians. Beginners may enjoy browsing, although the easiest song tutorials are only available to subscribers. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

FirstChord is a free guitar-learning website with short videos for complete beginners. Users can filter lessons by level and choose simple songs using only two or three chords. There are also slow practice tracks for playing along at home. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

News in Ten publishes short international news stories using simple language. Every report has a two-minute audio version and a vocabulary box. The site is updated daily and is popular with learners who want to understand current events without long articles. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions.

TechPriceCheck compares prices for laptops, screens and accessories. It includes customer reviews and graphs showing price changes over the last six months. Users can set alerts when an item becomes cheaper at several online shops. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

GardenPro Forum is a busy discussion board for experienced gardeners. Members share advice about rare plants and greenhouse equipment. There are many photographs, but beginners may find the technical language confusing and there is little information about pots. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

DailyWorldPlus offers detailed news analysis and opinion columns. Articles are carefully written but often very long. There are podcasts, although they are thirty minutes or more and do not include vocabulary help. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

answers.explanation: Ben needs short mobile maths exercises with instant answers; A matches exactly.
Clara needs beginner gardening advice, pictures and pot information; B fits best.
Daniel needs free beginner guitar videos and easy songs; D matches, unlike C.
Nina wants short simple news with audio; E provides both.
Tom needs price comparison, reviews and price-history information; F fits all requirements.
